faq.question: What is burnout?
faq.answer: Burnout is chronic workplace stress that has not been successfully managed. Symptoms include exhaustion, cynicism about work, reduced professional efficacy, and physical symptoms.

faq.question: What are the warning signs?
faq.answer: Dreading work, chronic fatigue, insomnia, irritability, difficulty concentrating, frequent illness, feeling detached, and declining performance.

faq.question: How do I recover from burnout?
faq.answer: Set boundaries on work hours, take your vacation time, prioritize sleep, exercise regularly, practice stress management, and consider professional help. Recovery takes weeks to months.

faq.question: Is burnout the same as depression?
faq.answer: They overlap but are distinct. Burnout is tied to work and improves with rest/vacation. Depression is pervasive and does not improve with time off alone. Both can coexist.

faq.question: Is the Burnout Risk Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Burnout Risk Calculator?
sections.body: The Burnout Risk Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals seeking balance who need quick, accurate calculations in the mental health and wellness space. By entering your weekly work hours, vacation days used this year, stress level, you get instant results including burnout risk score, risk level, biggest risk factor. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting burnout risk score right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In mental health and wellness, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals seeking balance worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to support your mental well-being with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your weekly work hours and need to find the right burnout risk score. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Weekly Work Hours
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your weekly work hours — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: vacation days used this year, stress level, avg sleep, exercise days/week. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your burnout risk score, risk level, biggest risk factor. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your weekly work hours before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.

If you're unsure about your exercise days/week, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
